title: [PS-67188] Fully restricted user from entering edit mode on drawing annotation labels
body:
## Changes:
* Locked content of annotation labels so user cannot enter edit mode at all on annotation labels rather than reactively kicking users out of edit mode when they double-click into a label
  * This also removed the need to close any popups since that no longer happens
* Fixed issue where measurement annotation labels were not updated when a user adjusted their size
author: henrykaus

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR updates the Apryse/PDFTron drawing label implementation to fully prevent users from entering edit mode on annotation labels (by locking label contents up front), and ensures measurement label text stays in sync when measurement annotations change size.

**Changes:**
- Set label annotations to `LockedContents = true` and stop relying on the prior “close style popup” double-click workaround.
- Update measurement label contents when a measurement annotation changes (so the displayed value stays current).
- Refactor label-content updates via a shared helper (`updateLabelInternal`).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| Core/Web Layer/WebUI/SharedPdftronExtensions.js | Locks label contents, updates measurement labels on parent changes, refactors label update logic. |
| Core/Web Layer/WebUI/DrawingAnnotationEdit_v7.js | Removes the legacy double-click workaround that forcibly closed the label style popup. |
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

review_comments.body: `LockedContents` is being cleared **after** `serializer.apply(...)` has already serialized the annotation, so it likely won’t be removed from the XFDF payload. It also permanently mutates the live annotation object, which can unintentionally drop the runtime “locked” permission enforcement for non-label annotations after any export/save. Consider temporarily clearing it before serialization and then restoring it afterward.
review_comments.author: Copilot
